header.description: Two-ship gunfire scenario pinning G1 ballistic projectiles (Step 7 guns, seal v1.9r0). AC0 (P-47D) settles into a banked climbing right turn (25 deg bank, 1.3 g) and fires a 20-round burst over ticks 100-119: each round leaves the muzzle at the firer's POST-step state (lat,lon,psi,alt,gamma) with speed = TAS + 850 m/s, so the rounds fan with the turning heading and inherit the climb's positive flight-path angle. AC1 (A6M2) pushes to a shallow dive (0.5 g) then fires a 15-round burst over ticks 150-164 with a slightly negative gamma. Each round is the n=0/thrust=0 specialization of the aircraft 3-DOF step: gravity bends gamma down, a lumped quadratic drag (k=2e-4) bleeds speed, the heading is fixed and the great-circle step carries the geodesic. ttl=250 ticks keeps all 35 rounds airborne at tick 300 (every round captured in the world_hash), and all stay well above ground (start alt >= 3000 m) and far inside +/-90 deg flight-path angle (no cos(gamma) singularity). NEW golden for G1. NOTE: 1 round/tick is a deterministic placeholder rate; real per-weapon fire-rate + roster are G3.
header.introduced: first golden to exercise the projectile snapshot block; the 6 prior goldens move at v1.9r0 only because the block (n=0 for them) is appended to every snapshot.
